---
title: "cleanmodel"
format: html
editor: visual
---

## 

```{r warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}
library(rmarkdown); library(knitr); library(igraph); library(corrplot); library(moments); library(readxl); library(dplyr); library(ggplot2); library(lubridate); library(leaflet); library(readr); library(leaflet.extras2); library(sf); library(janitor); library(broom);library(MASS);library(spdep)

```

**Aggregate weekly**

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(lubridate)
library(readxl)

# Step 1 — load data
shootings <- read_excel("Shootings_Full Data(new).xlsx", sheet = "Full Data")

# Step 2 — create individual-level variables
data_full <- shootings %>%
  arrange(DATE, Time) %>%
  mutate(
    DATE   = as.Date(DATE),
    month  = month(DATE),
    season = case_when(
      month %in% c(12, 1, 2) ~ "winter",
      month %in% c(3, 4, 5)  ~ "spring",
      month %in% c(6, 7, 8)  ~ "summer",
      TRUE                   ~ "fall"
    ),
    unit = ifelse(DATE >= as.Date("2024-01-01"), 1, 0),
    # ISO week label for grouping
    week_start = floor_date(DATE, unit = "week", week_start = 1)  # weeks start Monday
  )

# Step 3 — aggregate to weekly
weekly_data <- data_full %>%
  group_by(week_start) %>%
  summarise(
    y      = n(),                                         # total shootings that week
    unit   = max(unit),    # 1 if the unit was active at any point that week
    season = names(which.max(table(season))),             # majority season
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(week_start) %>%
  mutate(
    trend     = as.integer(week_start - min(week_start)) %/% 7L,  # week index: 0, 1, 2, ...
    lag_y     = lag(y, default = 0),
    lag_y_log = log(lag_y + 1)
  )

# Step 4 — fit Poisson model
weekly_model <- glm(
  y ~ trend + unit + factor(season) + lag_y_log,
  family = poisson(link = "log"),
  data   = weekly_data
)

# Step 5 — results
summary(weekly_model)
exp(coef(weekly_model))          # IRRs
AIC(weekly_model)
```

**Plots for weekly** **Model**

```{r}

```

**Reading Spacial Data**

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(sf)

unzip("StPaul_Police_Gri_LayerToKML.kmz", exdir = tempdir())
kml_path <- list.files(tempdir(), pattern = "\\.kml$", full.names = TRUE)
spatial_data <- st_read(kml_path)




# Check what you got
print(spatial_data)
st_crs(spatial_data)        # coordinate reference system
plot(st_geometry(spatial_data))  # quick visual check

```

**Joining Data**

```{r}

grid_counts <- data_full %>%
  group_by(GRID) %>%
  summarise(y = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(GRID = as.character(GRID))      # convert to match spatial_data$Name

grid_sf <- spatial_data %>%
  left_join(grid_counts, by = c("Name" = "GRID")) %>%
  m
utate(y = replace_na(y, 0))
```

**Space Model(only)**

```{r}
spatial_only_model <- glm(
  y ~ 1,                          # intercept only, spatial structure captured by weights
  family = poisson(link = "log"),
  data = grid_sf
)

grid_sf$residuals <- residuals(spatial_only_model, type = "pearson")
```

**Spatial Matrix**

```{r}


neighbors <- poly2nb(grid_sf, queen = TRUE)
weights   <- nb2listw(neighbors, style = "W", zero.policy = TRUE)
```

**Morans - I**

```{r}
moran_test <- moran.test(grid_sf$residuals, weights, zero.policy = TRUE)
print(moran_test)
```

**LISAS**

```{r}
lisa <- localmoran(grid_sf$residuals, weights, zero.policy = TRUE)

grid_sf <- grid_sf %>%
  mutate(
    lisa_I   = lisa[, 1],
    lisa_p   = lisa[, 5],
    lisa_sig = lisa_p < 0.05
  )

plot(grid_sf["lisa_I"], main = "LISA - Local Moran's I")
```

**Lisas with numbers on hot spots(whole data)**

```{r}
# Get centroids for label placement
grid_sf <- grid_sf %>%
  mutate(
    centroid_x = st_coordinates(st_centroid(geometry))[, 1],
    centroid_y = st_coordinates(st_centroid(geometry))[, 2]
  )

# Plot with grid numbers
ggplot(grid_sf) +
  geom_sf(aes(fill = lisa_I)) +
  geom_text(data = filter(grid_sf, lisa_sig == TRUE),
            aes(x = centroid_x, y = centroid_y, label = Name),
            size = 2.5, color = "black") +
  scale_fill_gradient2(low = "blue", mid = "white", high = "red",
                       midpoint = 0, name = "Local Moran's I") +
  labs(title = "LISA - Local Moran's I by Police Grid",
       subtitle = "Labels shown only for significant clusters (p < 0.05)") +
  theme_minimal()
```

**Before and After Spitting data**

```{r}
# Split data into before and after
before_sf <- data_full %>%
  filter(DATE < as.Date("2024-01-01")) %>%
  group_by(GRID) %>%
  summarise(y = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(GRID = as.character(GRID))

after_sf <- data_full %>%
  filter(DATE >= as.Date("2024-01-01")) %>%
  group_by(GRID) %>%
  summarise(y = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(GRID = as.character(GRID))

# Join both to spatial grid
grid_before <- spatial_data %>%
  left_join(before_sf, by = c("Name" = "GRID")) %>%
  mutate(y = replace_na(y, 0))

grid_after <- spatial_data %>%
  left_join(after_sf, by = c("Name" = "GRID")) %>%
  mutate(y = replace_na(y, 0))
```

**Poisons residual** **and Morans I** **before and after**

```{r}
# Poisson residuals for each period
grid_before$residuals <- residuals(
  glm(y ~ 1, family = poisson(link = "log"), data = grid_before), 
  type = "pearson"
)

grid_after$residuals <- residuals(
  glm(y ~ 1, family = poisson(link = "log"), data = grid_after), 
  type = "pearson"
)

# Weights matrix (same for both since grid geometry doesn't change)
neighbors <- poly2nb(grid_before, queen = TRUE)
weights   <- nb2listw(neighbors, style = "W", zero.policy = TRUE)

# Moran's I for each period
cat("--- BEFORE ---\n")
print(moran.test(grid_before$residuals, weights, zero.policy = TRUE))

cat("--- AFTER ---\n")
print(moran.test(grid_after$residuals, weights, zero.policy = TRUE))
```

**LISAS before and after**

```{r}
# LISA for each period
lisa_before <- localmoran(grid_before$residuals, weights, zero.policy = TRUE)
lisa_after  <- localmoran(grid_after$residuals,  weights, zero.policy = TRUE)

grid_before <- grid_before %>%
  mutate(
    lisa_I   = lisa_before[, 1],
    lisa_p   = lisa_before[, 5],
    lisa_sig = lisa_p < 0.05,
    centroid_x = st_coordinates(st_centroid(geometry))[, 1],
    centroid_y = st_coordinates(st_centroid(geometry))[, 2]
  )

grid_after <- grid_after %>%
  mutate(
    lisa_I   = lisa_after[, 1],
    lisa_p   = lisa_after[, 5],
    lisa_sig = lisa_p < 0.05,
    centroid_x = st_coordinates(st_centroid(geometry))[, 1],
    centroid_y = st_coordinates(st_centroid(geometry))[, 2]
  )

# Plot side by side
library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)

p1 <- ggplot(grid_before) +
  geom_sf(aes(fill = lisa_I)) +
  geom_text(data = filter(grid_before, lisa_sig == TRUE),
            aes(x = centroid_x, y = centroid_y, label = Name),
            size = 2.5, color = "black") +
  scale_fill_gradient2(low = "blue", mid = "white", high = "red",
                       midpoint = 0, name = "Local Moran's I",
                       limits = c(-3, 3)) +   # same scale for fair comparison
  labs(title = "Before Unit (pre Jan 2024)") +
  theme_minimal()

p2 <- ggplot(grid_after) +
  geom_sf(aes(fill = lisa_I)) +
  geom_text(data = filter(grid_after, lisa_sig == TRUE),
            aes(x = centroid_x, y = centroid_y, label = Name),
            size = 2.5, color = "black") +
  scale_fill_gradient2(low = "blue", mid = "white", high = "red",
                       midpoint = 0, name = "Local Moran's I",
                       limits = c(-3, 3)) +   # same scale for fair comparison
  labs(title = "After Unit (Jan 2024 onward)") +
  theme_minimal()

p1 + p2 + plot_layout(guides = "collect")

#The dark gray/black grids (87, 88, 89) before are extremely high LISA values that appear to cool slightly after — the intensity of the hot spot looks a bit more distributed rather than concentrated in those exact cells
#More grids are labeled after (31, 32, 33, 91, 114, 145, 163-167, 183-186, 206) — suggesting that after deployment, significant spatial clustering actually became detectable in more areas, possibly indicating some displacement of violence outward
#The southern grids (163-186 area) show up as labeled significant clusters only in the after map — this is worth paying attention to as a potential displacement signal
```

```{r}
library(surveillance)

fit <- hhh4(sts_object, control = list(  ar = list(f = ~ 1),  end = list(f = ~ x1 + x2 + offset(log(population))) ))
```

d
